title: Standards-mode document skeleton

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Standards-mode document skeleton; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A standards-mode document declares the HTML doctype and provides html, head, and body structure with language and metadata.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A page begins with <!doctype html>, sets lang on html, gives a title in head, and puts visible content in body. Your goal is to create and explain a complete minimal HTML document with early encoding declaration. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Elements, attributes and nesting. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A standards-mode document declares the HTML doctype and provides html, head, and body structure with language and metadata. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Browsers can infer omitted elements, but explicit structure supports tooling, accessibility, and predictable maintenance. This draft therefore rejects: The doctype is an optional comment that only documents the HTML version.

includes: Declare doctype, language, character encoding, viewport and body content correctly.
Create and explain a complete minimal HTML document with early encoding declaration.
excludes: Browsers can infer omitted elements, but explicit structure supports tooling, accessibility, and predictable maintenance.

outcomes: Create and explain a complete minimal HTML document with early encoding declaration.

walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A page begins with <!doctype html>, sets lang on html, gives a title in head, and puts visible content in body.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use <!doctype html> to request standards mode and provide explicit html, head, and body structure.
examples.result: The result follows because Use <!doctype html> to request standards mode and provide explicit html, head, and body structure.

Step 3 — Reject the misconception: The doctype is an optional comment that only documents the HTML version.
